news.title: Lift safety checks lag in Pune, Mumbai divisions as recent mishaps in Nashik sound alarm bells
news.para: Lift safety inspections are alarmingly low in Pune and parts of Mumbai, with only 25.62% compliance in Pune city. Recent fatal incidents in Nashik highlight the risks of missed mandatory annual checks. Officials cite staff shortages and increasing workloads as key challenges, despite state-level announcements of new inspector posts and improved procedures.

news.title: Dehradun administration cancels registration of 17 illegal homestays
news.para: Dehradun district administration has cancelled registrations of 17 homestays in Sahaspur and Raipur as part of 'Operation Safai' due to violations. Inspections revealed issues like lack of proper facilities, fire safety lapses, and unauthorized commercial use. This action aims to ensure homestays adhere to regulations and promote local culture.

news.title: Accenture leases over six lakh sq ft in Pune, plans GCC in long-term ₹325 crore deal
news.para: Accenture has secured a significant office space of over 600,000 sq ft in Pune. This new facility will house a global capability centre. The occupation will occur in two phases, concluding by June. This expansion is part of a larger strategy to enhance operational efficiency and consolidate teams.

news.title: Co-working spaces trigger GST registration hurdles
news.para: Businesses using co-working spaces in Gujarat are facing increased GST registration scrutiny, with tax authorities questioning the validity of shared premises. Applicants are being asked for proof of exclusive possession, leading to rejections due to multiple firms at one address. Experts advise clearer guidelines and robust documentation for flexible workspaces.

news.title: Haryana: DTCP orders statewide audit of infrastructure, funds
news.para: The Department of Town and Country Planning is collecting data on infrastructure, encroachments, and funds. This follows a High Court order concerning the stilt-plus-four-floor policy. Urban agencies must submit detailed reports soon. The court is assessing if infrastructure can support increased density. Approvals for S+4 floors are currently halted. This exercise will inform future policy decisions.

news.title: Dubai eases visa rule for single property buyers
news.para: Dubai has removed the minimum property value for its two-year residency visa for individual buyers. This move aims to boost realty demand. For jointly owned properties, the minimum stake per investor is now AED 400,000. This change is expected to attract mid-tier professionals. The decision follows a significant slump in real estate sales volumes in Dubai.

news.title: Telangana government signs agreement with L&T for acquisition of 100% equity shares of LTMRHL
news.para: The Telangana government has successfully acquired complete ownership of Hyderabad Metro Rail Phase I. They inked a Share Purchase Agreement with L&T, securing 100 percent equity shares in a deal worth Rs 1,461.47 crores.

news.title: Kone strikes $34 billion deal to buy TK Elevator and create world's largest lift maker
news.para: Finnish lift maker Kone is set to acquire German rival TK Elevator for 29.4 billion euros. This deal will create the world's largest lift maker. Kone aims to expand its presence in the Americas. The merger is expected to yield significant cost savings. Regulatory approvals are anticipated to take 12 to 18 months.

news.title: Our objective is to give houses to 22,000 homebuyers of ailing Unitech: SC
news.para: The Supreme Court has taken decisive action to resolve the plight of 22,000 Unitech homebuyers by appointing a new board to oversee the troubled real estate firm. Seeking collaborative solutions, the court has asked for input from the board, financial institutions, and banks. The ultimate aim is to facilitate the delivery of homes to buyers who have been left waiting.

news.title: Brigade Group & Bain Capital to jointly develop two million sq ft project in Bengaluru
news.para: Brigade Group and Bain Capital are joining forces for a major development in Whitefield, Bengaluru. This 50:50 joint venture will invest approximately ₹2,200 crore. The project will span 11 acres, featuring two million sq ft of grade-A office space and a five-star hotel. It recently acquired land in Osman Nagar, Hyderabad for a residential project.

news.title: Mindspace Business Parks REIT's net operating income up 29.2% in FY26
news.para: Mindspace REIT announces 29.2 percent growth in net operating income for the financial year 2025-26. The company's board has approved raising funds up to ₹15,700 crore. A distribution of ₹6.64 per unit has been declared for the quarter ending March 31, 2026.

news.title: Ramky Estates takes over stalled Skylark projects in Bengaluru, secures ₹600 crore SWAMIH funding
news.para: Ramky Estates has taken over stalled residential projects spanning 4.3 million sq ft in Bengaluru from Skylark Builders, with ₹600 crore funding from the SWAMIH Fund to support completion. The projects have an estimated revenue potential of ₹2,000 crore.

news.title: County Group acquires two stalled housing projects in Gurugram
news.para: County Group has acquired two stalled housing projects in Gurgaon, offering relief to nearly 400 homebuyers. The acquisitions provide the developer with 2.6 million sq ft of saleable area and an estimated revenue potential of Rs 4,000 crore. This move aims to revive projects that have been delayed for over a decade.

news.title: ETRealty Awards 2026 winner Open Greens sets a new benchmark for future-ready workspaces
news.para: Open Greens in Noida has been honored with two ET Realty Awards for its innovative commercial design. Nestled in Sector 136, this development boasts eco-friendly architecture and cutting-edge infrastructure, ensuring seamless access for businesses.

news.title: How MahaRERA's circular 50/2025 changes the cancellation deadlock and the questions it leaves behind
news.para: A recent Bombay High Court ruling and a new MahaRERA circular provide a crucial solution for developers facing payment defaults. Previously, stalled property titles were a major issue. Now, MahaRERA can appoint an officer to execute cancellation deeds on behalf of non-compliant allottees. This streamlines the process, allowing developers to clear encumbrances and improve project cash flow.

news.title: Inorbit malls to acquire Prozone Group malls in Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar, Coimbatore
news.para: Inorbit Malls is set to acquire two malls from Prozone Group for approximately Rs 1,300 crore. This strategic move will significantly expand Inorbit's retail footprint by adding 1.2 million square feet. The acquisition positions Inorbit to compete with major players in India's growing retail sector. Demand for retail assets in tier two cities remains strong.

news.title: Neo Alternative Asset Managers commits ₹300 crore to Hyderabad residential project
news.para: Neo Alternative Asset Managers has committed ₹300 crore in structured debt to a premium residential project in Hyderabad’s Narsingi, marking its first real estate investment in the city and underscoring growing institutional interest in West Hyderabad.

news.title: Why does wall putty crack and how can you prevent it?
news.para: Birla White Wall Care Putty doesn't just make walls smooth; it actually makes them stronger.  Cracks usually happen because of bad application, dampness, or cheap materials. Using the right method and a good product, like Birla White WallCare Putty, is key.  For walls that stay smooth and crack-free for ages, pick a reliable, high-quality putty.

news.title: Strata exits Bengaluru industrial asset for ₹35 crore
news.para: Strata has sold an industrial asset in Bengaluru for ₹35.3 crore, delivering a 15.3% internal rate of return over five years. The transaction reflects continued investor interest in income-generating industrial assets with long-term leases.

news.title: Omaxe to invest ₹250 crore in residential project in Lucknow; Brigade acquires 5.72-acre land in Hyderabad
news.para: Omaxe Garv Buildtech will invest ₹250 crore in a new residential project in Lucknow. The Omaxe Cassia project will offer 2BHK and 3BHK apartments. Meanwhile, Brigade Enterprises has bought land in Hyderabad. They plan to build a residential project on this site.
